steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a vertical line from top to bottom of where the birdbath will stand. Every part — basin, pedestal, base — will balance around this axis.

steps.title: Block the basin (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a wide, shallow ellipse at the top for the basin's rim. Below it, sketch a second tighter curve for the underside of the bowl, narrowing toward the pedestal.

steps.title: Block the pedestal and base (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a narrow column running down the axis from the bowl. At the bottom, add a wider trapezoid or stepped block for the base. Compare the heights — the bowl, pedestal, and base each take up roughly a third.

steps.title: Refine the silhouette (pencil)
steps.description: Look at where the bowl meets the pedestal — those are tight curves. Where the pedestal meets the base — also tight. The sides of the pedestal itself can be gently bowed rather than straight. Erase and redraw any line that feels off.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Trace the full birdbath outline in confident strokes, landing the lines cleanly at the bowl–pedestal and pedestal–base junctions. Stop the back rim of the basin where the front rim crosses it — don't draw all the way through.

steps.title: Ink the water and stone texture (ink)
steps.description: Inside the basin, add a small flat ellipse to suggest water sitting in the bowl. On the pedestal and base, add a few short marks suggesting the seams between stone blocks — a few are enough.

steps.title: Anchor with a shadow (ink)
steps.description: Add a flattened shadow shape on the tiles just to one side of the base. A simple wedge or ellipse grounds the birdbath on the floor instead of leaving it floating.
